title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-engineers can ask business questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion in natural language. The system generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, enabling deterministic and cost-efficient query execution while avoiding repetitive manual requests.
writeup: Garrett Galow of WorkOS describes Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-engineers ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. Rather than re-running ad hoc queries, the system generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, which makes query execution deterministic and cost-efficient. The talk covers the supporting machinery: context management, preflight sequencing, query validation, prompt layering, and retrieval-augmented generation. The core takeaway is that compiling natural language into persistent widgets reduces repetitive manual data requests while keeping results reliable.
